_comment: Ranker-regression expectations over data/seed.json + data/cooccurrence.json. CHARACTERIZATION values (what the deterministic engine produces for the default JOURNEY archetype at the trust>=0.50 gate), not hand-picked winners. Update deliberately when the seed, co-occurrence, or scoring changes, and record why. History: science subgraph -> Mithra; Hellenistic-India-Buddhism bridge (0011); default cap cut 6->4 (0012); breadth clusters Greece (0016), Egypt (0017), Islamic Golden Age (0018), Scientific Revolution (0019), East Asia (0020); journey cap cut 4->3 (0021, fixed-length 3-hop journeys); Norse/Celtic (0022); Chinese tech (0023); second-order co-occurrence (0025), reworked by full-link Jaccard similarity (0029) which measures shared context over each article's whole outbound link set rather than the seed-sized keyhole -- this de-saturated the term (max tie-fraction 94% -> 1.1%) and re-ordered the endpoints again. ADR 0034 weighted each domain jump by its unexpectedness given the predicate (a flat count paid full price for tautological crossings like located_in->geography) and ADR 0035 closed the 24 open-ended temporal extents (a still-standing city founded in 59 BC no longer reads as an ancient node). ADR 0038 added the South/SE Asia cluster (9 nodes / 17 statements): the new edges shifted predicate-level rarity globally, re-ordering two winners hair's-breadth apart -- Roman Empire's top journey flipped Qin Shi Huang (via Silk Road -> Great Wall) -> Plato (via Ancient Greece -> Renaissance humanism), 38.02 vs 37.65, and Euclid's terminus moved from Persia to the new Maurya Empire (Euclid -> Alexandria -> Alexander the Great -> Maurya Empire). Christianity -> Paper is unchanged. ADR 0039 added the region-jump surprise term (W_REGION=2.0), an additive cultural-sphere axis alongside domain: it reinforced the cross-cultural science lineage (Copernicus -> al-Tusi -> Euclid -> Jagannatha now clearly #1) and pushed Western-canon walking tours out of the top results, re-characterising two winners again -- Roman Empire's top journey is now Roman Empire -> Silk Road -> Buddhism -> Zen (a genuine trans-Eurasian route; the Qin Shi Huang and Plato routes rank just behind), and Christianity's is Christianity -> Roman Empire -> Silk Road -> Zhang Qian (Christianity is regioned near_eastern, so its spread into the Roman world now scores). Euclid -> Maurya Empire is unchanged. ADR 0041 added the active-period (floruit) temporal axis on Node: midpoint_year (and thus the temporal_gap term) now keys off a node's active period when present, falling back to the existence extent -- so a long-lived node reads its era of influence (India 300, Rome-the-city -138, Florence 1450) rather than a midpoint stretched to the present (India was -638). Measured: 11 of 107 journey winners shifted, all toward more trans-regional/trans-temporal destinations (e.g. Florence -> Renaissance -> printing press -> Paper, replacing a Western walking tour); all three golden cases here (Roman Empire -> Zen, Christianity -> Zhang Qian, Euclid -> Maurya Empire) are UNCHANGED. ADR 0042 added a curated per-statement `headline` and made the TIL the payoff (last) hop's headline (the improbable pair also became the default archetype) -- narration only, so ranking and all three cases were UNCHANGED. ADR 0043 added the Judaism/Abrahamic-web cluster (9 nodes / 17 statements: Judaism, Hebrew Bible, Jerusalem, Abraham, Moses, Second Temple, David, United Monarchy, Talmud): the new edges gave Christianity routes into its own Abrahamic and Roman roots, so its top journey shifted Zhang Qian -> Roman Republic (christianity -> jerusalem -> roman_empire -> roman_republic; the new Jerusalem->Rome region-jump plus a genuinely-unexpected Roman Republic endpoint, eu 7.90 -- not a farmed jump). Roman Empire -> Zen and Euclid -> Maurya Empire are UNCHANGED; the science lineage (Copernicus) and the divine-descent lineages (Elizabeth II, Naruhito) held. Re-characterise after any seed/co-occurrence/scoring change; winners were re-characterised from the engine -- not hand-picked.
